entries.name: Commander

license.notes: Repository mirror does not include a standalone license file or explicit license grant.

absorption.rationale: Useful product reference for local multi-agent orchestration, Git worktrees, and desktop execution controls.
blockedBy: no clear open-source license grant in mirrored repository

entries.summary: Native Tauri multi-agent desktop workspace with Git worktree orchestration, project launcher, and provider settings.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Added a stronger reference point for how far OSM should push local worktree orchestration and desktop session control.
constraints: Treat as reference-only until licensing is clarified.

entries.name: agentapi

absorption.rationale: Directly relevant to OSM's missing HTTP, SSE, and attach-style control plane.

entries.summary: HTTP and SSE control layer for agent processes with web chat surface and running-agent attachment.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Confirmed that OSM should grow beyond a GUI into a programmatic agent control surface.

entries.name: claude_code_tools

license.notes: Repository includes a BSD-2-Clause Plus Patent license.

absorption.rationale: Very close to OSM's export-before-delete workflow because it formalizes session closure, session resume, and git worktree handoff scripts.

entries.summary: Skill and hook pack for Claude Code with session closure/resume, git worktree automation, and context statusline tooling.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Pushed OSM to turn export-before-delete into an actual handoff workflow instead of a raw transcript dump.

entries.name: claude-code-viewer

absorption.rationale: Strong fit for deep transcript rendering, schema validation, and lossless detail inspection.

entries.summary: Specialized viewer for Claude Code with strong transcript fidelity, schema validation, and deep detail panes.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Helped shape OSM's viewer-style transcript detail panels and todo evidence workflow.
adoptedCapabilities: Session todo evidence in detail view

entries.name: claude-code-log

absorption.rationale: Very close to OSM's export-before-delete requirement and summary-centric cleanup flow.

entries.summary: Export-focused toolchain for Claude Code sessions with Markdown, HTML, and smart summary workflows.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Helped shape OSM's Markdown export and summary-first cleanup workflow.

entries.name: coding_agent_session_search

license.notes: Repository carries additional restrictions beyond a standard permissive license.

absorption.rationale: Its breadth of connector coverage is strategically useful, but the license posture blocks direct code reuse.

entries.summary: Broad multi-agent search tooling that proves the scale of connector coverage and export patterns OSM must beat.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Helped validate how much connector breadth OSM needs, but its code remains reference-only.
constraints: Do not copy code into the public OSM distribution.
Treat as reference-only until a clean-room replacement exists.

entries.name: Entropic

absorption.rationale: Cross-platform maintenance UX is directly useful for OSM's governance dashboard and cleanup flows.

entries.summary: Cross-platform desktop maintenance console that already thinks in provider-aware views and cleanup actions.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Validated that a cross-platform governance console has real product demand beyond a single assistant.

entries.name: Rover

absorption.rationale: Directly relevant to isolated execution, background tasks, and inspect-merge workflows that OSM still lacks.

entries.summary: Container-isolated coding agent runner with background task lifecycle, inspect/diff/merge loop, and VSCode extension hooks.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Raised the bar for how OSM should handle isolated background task execution and iteration workflows.

entries.name: cc-switch

absorption.rationale: Strong reference for provider, proxy, MCP, prompts, and skills governance that OSM still lacks.

entries.summary: Desktop switchboard for provider presets, MCP, prompts, skills, usage, proxy failover, and session management.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Clarified the configuration and provider governance surface OSM still needs to absorb.

entries.name: agent-sessions

license.notes: Repository page and README describe the project as MIT-licensed.

absorption.rationale: Strong alignment with OSM's local-first indexing and session analytics direction.

entries.summary: Desktop-first local session explorer with deep indexing, search, analytics, and broad assistant parser coverage.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Helped shape OSM's local-first session indexing direction and the multi-assistant adapter expansion.

entries.name: tokscale

absorption.rationale: Best reference currently mirrored for token, model, and cost analytics across coding agents.

entries.summary: Usage and cost analytics toolkit with wide connector matrix, pricing lookup, and contribution-style visualizations.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Established the analytics baseline OSM needs for token and cost observability.

entries.name: ccmanager

absorption.rationale: Strong reference for multi-project worktree orchestration around local coding agents.

entries.summary: Worktree-centric manager for multiple coding agents, project queues, and session state switching.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Clarified the level of worktree and multi-project orchestration OSM still needs.

entries.name: agentpipe

absorption.rationale: Useful reference for multi-agent rooms, metrics, doctor checks, and multi-format export.

entries.summary: Multi-agent coordination framework with metrics, token tracking, doctor checks, and JSON/Markdown/HTML export.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Showed how OSM can grow toward multi-agent health checks, metrics, and richer export surfaces.

entries.name: CodexFlow

absorption.rationale: Directly relevant to Windows and WSL multi-assistant orchestration, which is central to OSM's target users.

entries.summary: Electron-based multi-assistant workspace focused on Windows, WSL, and project-centric history.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Validated OSM's Windows and WSL-first direction for multi-assistant session management.

entries.name: Codexia

license.notes: Repository includes GNU AGPL v3 license text.

absorption.rationale: Important product reference for headless web server, realtime event streaming, and integrated workbench UX.

entries.summary: Agent IDE and workbench with headless server, WebSocket streaming, file previews, MCP marketplace, and remote control API.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Expanded OSM's target surface for headless serving, realtime events, and integrated workspace tooling.
constraints: Treat as reference-only unless a clean-room implementation is maintained.

entries.name: claude-code-tools

absorption.rationale: Strong reference for plugins, hooks, safety workflows, session repair, and search tooling around Claude Code.

entries.summary: Productivity toolkit for Claude Code with plugins, hooks, safety helpers, search binary, session repair, and provider integrations.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Gave OSM a clearer path toward skills, hooks, repair workflows, and extensible session tooling.

entries.name: claudecodeui

license.notes: Repository includes GPL v3 license text.

absorption.rationale: Important reference for responsive remote UI, integrated shell, file explorer, REST API, and plugin system.

entries.summary: Remote-capable web UI for coding agents with session management, shell, file explorer, git explorer, REST API, and plugins.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Raised the bar for remote, multi-device, and plugin-friendly surfaces OSM still lacks.
constraints: Treat as reference-only unless a clean-room implementation is maintained.

entries.name: claude-squad

license.notes: Repository includes GNU AGPL v3 license text.

absorption.rationale: Useful reference for tmux/worktree multi-session orchestration, attach/detach, pause/resume, and review-before-apply flows.

entries.summary: tmux and worktree based multi-session orchestration toolkit with attach/detach, review-before-apply, and profile launching.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Clarified how much session control and pause/resume orchestration OSM still lacks.
constraints: Treat as reference-only unless a clean-room implementation is maintained.

entries.name: Gemini CLI Web UI

license.notes: README claims GPL lineage, LICENSE file is GPL-3.0, but package metadata advertises MIT; treat as license-conflicted until clarified.

absorption.rationale: Strong reference for remote Gemini workspace features, auth, WebSocket streaming, spec workflows, and integrated file/git/shell panels, but the license posture is currently contradictory.
blockedBy: conflicting license metadata between LICENSE, README, and package metadata
public distribution risk until the upstream clarifies reuse terms

entries.summary: Responsive Gemini CLI web workspace with auth, WebSocket chat, file and git explorers, shell panel, and spec-design tooling.
stackSignals: embedded editor, git, shell, and spec tooling
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Raised the bar for Gemini-specific remote workspace breadth, but remains reference-only until licensing is clear.
constraints: Do not copy code into OSM while the license conflict remains unresolved.

entries.name: claude-code-webui

absorption.rationale: Useful clean-room reference for lightweight remote browser access, permission-mode UI, session continuity, and single-binary backend packaging.

entries.summary: Lightweight Claude browser shell with NDJSON streaming backend, project history loading, plan-mode approval UX, and single-binary distribution.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Added a concrete reference for lightweight remote access, session continuity, and browser-native approval UX.

entries.name: dotai

absorption.rationale: Not a direct product competitor, but highly relevant for workflow plugins, debugging disciplines, and skill automation.

entries.summary: Workflow and plugin framework for AI-assisted development with structured debug, test, learn, and research helpers.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Added a methodology reference for turning session knowledge into reusable workflows and skills.

entries.name: claude-code-web

absorption.rationale: Useful reference for remote browser access, auth, multi-session persistence, WebSocket streaming, and split views.

entries.summary: Web interface for Claude Code with authentication, multi-session persistence, split views, REST API, and WebSocket streaming.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Added a stronger benchmark for remote browser access and multi-session persistence.

entries.name: ai-sessions-mcp

absorption.rationale: Useful for exposing local session knowledge into MCP-compatible automation without coupling to the UI shell.

entries.summary: TypeScript MCP server that exposes local AI coding sessions for retrieval and automation workflows.
entries.releaseAcknowledgement: Helped confirm that OSM should expose a headless session retrieval surface beyond the GUI.
